mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances despite the documented unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to yield positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of overlapping monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the consumer price index erode real corporate profit margins and trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently precede positive 5-day SPX returns with a Rank IC exceeding 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive months of nonfarm payrolls growth above 200,000 confirm robust economic expansion and rising household income, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k per month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: The 200k payroll threshold fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered months across the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth validate synchronized demand across production and consumption sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of overlapping quarterly and monthly periods.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, each incremental increase in the federal funds rate compresses equity multiples and raises discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during tightening cycles produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, invalidating the discount rate compression effect.
